seoTitle: How to Update and Refresh Data in Power BI
metaDescription: Keep your Power BI dashboard current by learning local refresh triggers, scheduled cloud refresh, and gateway setup so metrics never go stale.
intro: Nothing undermines a team dashboard faster than stale numbers that don't reflect this morning's updates. Power BI gives you both a manual refresh trigger for local development and an automated scheduled refresh pipeline for published dashboards. This guide covers how to set up both so your visuals stay accurate without constant manual work.

steps.heading: Trigger a manual refresh in Desktop
steps.body: Use the Refresh button on the Home ribbon in Power BI Desktop to immediately pull updated data and recalculate your visuals while you're actively working on a report.

steps.heading: Publish your report to the Power BI Service
steps.body: Once your report is ready, publish it to the cloud service so it can be shared and refreshed independently of your local machine.

steps.heading: Configure data source credentials
steps.body: In the Power BI Service, set up secure credentials for each data source your report depends on, which is required before scheduled refresh can run automatically.

steps.heading: Set up a data gateway if needed
steps.body: For on-premises data sources, install and configure a gateway so the cloud service can securely reach data that isn't natively cloud-hosted.

steps.heading: Schedule automatic refresh
steps.body: Configure a daily or weekly refresh schedule in the dataset settings, and check the refresh history if a scheduled run fails so you can troubleshoot quickly.

faq.question: How often can I schedule Power BI to refresh automatically?
faq.answer: This depends on your license tier, but scheduled refresh commonly supports multiple refreshes per day on paid plans.

faq.question: Why is my scheduled refresh failing?
faq.answer: Failures are most often caused by expired or incorrect data source credentials, or a gateway that isn't running, so check the refresh history for specific error details.

faq.question: Do I need a gateway if all my data is already in the cloud?
faq.answer: No, a gateway is only required when connecting to on-premises or local network data sources.

faq.question: Does refreshing in Desktop update the published cloud report too?
faq.answer: No, refreshing locally in Desktop only updates what you see there; you need to republish or rely on the cloud's own scheduled refresh to update the published version.
